libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment variable is needed. Tests that set Log4j-related system properties should restore them after each test and should avoid relying on ambient logging configuration.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The compatibility bridge delegates meaningful Log4j 1.x logging/configuration behavior to Log4j 2 Core; in 2.18.0 PropertySetter references Log4j1Configuration.DEFAULT_LEVEL, whose class depends on log4j-core classes, so tests without core can silently fail conversion paths instead of covering norma
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6772 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-1.2-api:2.18.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: log4j-core is optional in the published POM, so adding it intentionally broadens the test classpath to the normal runtime/backend setup rather than the minimal API-only setup.
If tests cover optional integrations such as JMS appenders, further optional APIs may be needed, but they are not required for baseline meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: log4j-1.2-api 2.18.0 has core-dependent compatibility paths: the artifact declares log4j-core as optional, but normal Log4j 1.x migration/use requires a Log4j 2 backend and 2.18.0 classes such as Log4j1Configuration are referenced from PropertySetter/configuration code.
